title: Wynn Resorts Announces Second Quarter Earnings Release Date
text: LAS VEGAS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wynn Resorts, Limited (NASDAQ: WYNN) announced today that it will release the Company’s financial results for the second quarter ended June 30, 2022 after the market close on Tuesday, August 9, 2022, followed by a conference call at 2:00 p.m. PT (5:00 p.m. ET). The call will be broadcast live at www.wynnresorts.com under the “Company Information” section. Interested parties may also dial (888) 455-5965 or, for international callers, (773) 799-3869. The conference call access code is 3983556. A replay of the call will be available through September 9, 2022 by dialing (800) 925-0735 or, for international callers, (203) 369-3409. The replay access code is 3983556. The call will also be archived at www.wynnresorts.com.

title: Turning Point In Zero-Covid Policy Makes Wynn Resorts A Speculative Buy
text: I am re-rating Wynn Resorts from a sell to a buy. China is potentially at a turning point in its zero-Covid policy with a strong possibility of the country easing restrictions.

title: Macau Is Set to Reopen Casinos. Wynn and Las Vegas Sands Stocks Rise.
text: Casinos will be allowed to resume operations on July 23 in Macau after a 12-day shutdown, the government says.

title: 7 Stocks to Sell in a Recession
text: These stocks to sell in a recession need to be discarded from your portfolio to minimize your downside risks. The post 7 Stocks to Sell in a Recession appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: Wynn Las Vegas Named Best Hotel in Las Vegas for Third Consecutive Year by Travel + Leisure Magazine
text: The Resort Received Superior Ratings for Rooms, Location, Service, Food and More LAS VEGAS , July 12, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Wynn Las Vegas (Nasdaq: WYNN) was once again named the No. 1 hotel in Las Vegas on the Travel + Leisure 2022 World's Best Awards list, for the third consecutive year.

title: Casino Stocks Plummet After Macau Declares Shutdown
text: Stocks like MGM Resorts (MGM), Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO), Wynn Resorts (WYNN) and Las Vegas Sands (LVS) decline after Macau announces shutdown.

title: Big US casino operators like Wynn and MGM stand to lose millions after China's gambling capital of Macau shut down operations to curb Covid
text: China's gambling capital has shut down operations for the first time since February 2020 in an attempt to curb its worst outbreak of Covid-19 yet.

title: Shutdown: Why Casino Stocks Dropped Like a Rock Today
text: Macao's casinos could be in for another extended closure.

title: Macau Lockdown Slashes Targets For Las Vegas Sands, MGM, Wynn — Try These 3 US Casino Stocks Instead
text: Gambling stocks traded sharply lower on Monday morning as Macau casinos shut down for a week. In response, one Wall Street analyst cut his price targets for U.S.-listed casino stocks with exposure to Macau.

title: Why Citigroup Is Feeling Lucky on These Casino Stocks
text: Casino stocks were a staple of many portfolios for years, and with the expansion into Macau, China, they were considered a cash cow.

title: Here's Why You Should Retain Wynn Resorts' (WYNN) Stock
text: Wynn Resorts' (WYNN) focus on expansion into new market bodes well. However, subdued visitation in Macau is a concern.

title: Australia's Crown Resorts names Wynn Macau COO as top boss
text: Australian casino operator Crown Resorts on Thursday said Ciarán Carruthers, chief operating officer at Wynn Macau, would succeed Steve McCann as its chief executive in September.

title: What's Happening With Wynn Stock?
text: Wynn stock has declined by almost 32% year-to-date, considerably underperforming the S&P 500, which remains down by about 20% over the same period.
